title: Refactor OCP-011 (OCP): The 'print' method contains a large switch statement dispatching on 'ResultWa...
apply_error: syntax check failed: unresolved patch/conflict marker '=======' in extras/catch_amalgamated.cpp

detected_issue.description: The 'print' method contains a large switch statement dispatching on 'ResultWas::OfType'.
detected_issue.reasoning: Adding a new type of assertion result (new 'ResultWas::OfType' enum value) would require modifying this switch statement to add a new case, violating the Open/Closed Principle. This behavior should ideally be extended through polymorphism or a strategy pattern, where each result type knows how to print itself or has a dedicated printer strategy.

fix_logic.summary: Refactor TapAssertionPrinter to use a map-based dispatch for assertion results
fix_logic.rationale: Replaced the large switch statement in TapAssertionPrinter::print with a static map of handler functions. This decouples the printing logic from the ResultWas enum, allowing new assertion types to be added without modifying the core printing loop, thus adhering to the Open/Closed Principle.
